senses.definition: people who live in or frequent slums, often used to refer to residents of slum areas or those who adopt a lifestyle associated with poverty or deprivation
examples: The city surveys showed a growing number of slummers living near the riverbank.
Academic researchers studied the habits of slummers in the late 19th century.

senses.definition: more like a slum; more dirty or degraded in condition
examples: The alley looked slummier after the rain.
Our old neighborhood became slummier as funding declined.

senses.definition: most like a slum; having the poorest conditions
examples: The slummiest district was trying to attract investment.
They moved from a suburban area to the slummiest part of the city.

senses.definition: the quality of being like a slum; poor, dirty, or degraded conditions
examples: The slumminess of the alley deterred visitors.
Media coverage highlighted the slumminess of the housing project.

senses.definition: to visit or live in a poorer area for exploration, curiosity, or status signaling; to deliberately experience poverty or deprivation
examples: They were accused of slumming for a photo op.
She spent the weekend slumming in the backstreets to understand hardship.

senses.definition: instances of slumming; visits or stays in slum-like conditions
examples: The project documented several slummings across the district.
Her experiences during the slummings influenced her charity work.

senses.definition: a low, rough, or slovenly person or thing; a petty nuisance
examples: The old fence was a slummock of neglect.
Don’t let that slummock distract you from the main task.

senses.definition: having been treated as a slummock; shabbily treated or neglected
examples: The slummocked building stood ignored for years.
They replaced the slummocked furniture with something sturdier.

senses.definition: the act of performing or acting in a slummock manner; neglecting or debasing something through slovenliness
examples: The team was accused of slummocking the project timelines.
Slummocking the area further harmed community trust.

senses.definition: pieces or bits of slummock; neglected or shabby fragments
examples: The garden was cluttered with slummocks of old wood.
He picked through the debris, finding slummocks here and there.

senses.definition: slovenly, shabbily made or looking; of poor quality
examples: The slummocky furniture fell apart quickly.
They avoided slummocky venues for the event.

senses.definition: resembling a slum; dirty or poverty-stricken
examples: The alley looked slummy after the rain.
Some people live in slummy conditions despite city efforts.

senses.definition: to drop or fall suddenly and heavily
examples: Prices slump after a bad quarter.
She slumped into her chair from exhaustion.

senses.definition: a sudden fall or drop; a period of decline
examples: There was a slump in demand.
The unemployment rate saw a slump in the autumn.

senses.definition: past tense of slump; to have fallen or collapsed suddenly
examples: He slumped in his chair after hearing the news.
The stock price slumped yesterday.

senses.definition: a situation where inflation and a slump (economic stagnation) happen at the same time
examples: Experts debated whether the country was entering a period of slumpflation.
Policy makers watched signs of slumpflation in the housing market.

senses.definition: relating to or characterized by slumpflation
examples: The forecast includes slumpflationary pressures.
They implemented slumpflationary measures to ease the burden.

senses.definition: instances or periods of slumpflation
examples: Several economies experienced multiple slumpflations in the decade.
The report catalogues several slumpflations across sectors.

senses.definition: more likely to slump; more prone to decline
examples: Demand is slumpier this quarter than last year.
The market became slumpier as uncertainty grew.

senses.definition: most prone to slump; most depressed or declined
examples: The slumpiest sectors were housing and automotive.
Investors avoided the slumpiest stocks.

senses.definition: currently declining or falling steadily
examples: Sales are slumping as consumer confidence drops.
The team is slumping after a string of losses.

senses.definition: able to resist slumping or decline; resistant to becoming a slump
examples: The new model is slumpproof against market shocks.
They marketed it as slumpproof during uncertain times.

senses.definition: a feature or property that helps resist slumping
examples: The software includes a slumpproof algorithm.
Investors liked the slumpproof aspect of the project.

senses.definition: designed to prevent slum-like degradation; durable and reliable
examples: The building is slumproof and meant to last decades.
We need slumproof housing in high-density areas.

senses.definition: plural of slump; multiple declines or drops
examples: There were several slumps in the market this year.
The data shows recurring slumps in quarterly sales.

senses.definition: work that has declined in quality or vigor; work done during a slump
examples: The contractor complained about slumpwork on the project.
During the slump, many workers produced slumpwork rather than high-quality output.

senses.definition: unenergetic or sludgy; leaning toward slump; not lively
examples: He looked slumpy after the long flight.
The team had a slumpy mood in the locker room.
